title: README rewrite: full v2.0 narrative replacing rc.1 placeholder banner
description: Fully rewrite README.md replacing the rc.1 5-line v2 banner placeholder (commit fab90d4) with the v2.0 narrative. Tagline B': 'Fabric — cross-client knowledge for AI agents.' Pain-point opening (3-5 sentences explaining what Fabric solves). ASCII architecture diagram in first 100 words showing clients (Claude Code / Cursor / Codex) → MCP server → .fabric tree (knowledge/ + pending/ + .archive/ + agents.meta.json + events.jsonl). 'Why Fabric' section explaining metaphor evolution from AGENTS.md era (rule-binder) to knowledge-sustainment era. Quick start: single command `pnpm dlx @fenglimg/fabric-cli init`. Links to docs/knowledge-types.md, docs/initialization.md, docs/roadmap.md, CHANGELOG.md.

files.change: Section structure: (L1) `# Fabric — cross-client knowledge for AI agents.` (B' tagline as h1). (L3-7) Pain-point opening: 3-5 sentences (AGENTS.md era → fragile rule walls → 'I told you that 3 sessions ago' frustration → Fabric's answer = knowledge sustainment protocol). (L9-30) ASCII architecture diagram showing 3 clients → MCP server → .fabric tree with all 5 directories + 2 metadata files; placed within first 100 words of post-tagline content. (L32-50) 'Why Fabric' section: metaphor evolution paragraph (rule-binder → AGENTS.md → knowledge-sustainment); what Fabric does differently (5-type schema, 3-maturity, dual-root, lifecycle/lint moat, MCP-first cross-client, async review). (L52-58) Quick start: `pnpm dlx @fenglimg/fabric-cli init` single command + 1-line install verify (`fabric doctor`). (L60-80) What you get section: bulleted list of 4 MCP tools (fab_plan_context, fab_get_rule_sections, fab_extract_knowledge, fab_review) + 3 Skills (fabric-archive, fabric-review, fabric-import) + Stop hooks across 3 clients + dual-root layout + lifecycle/lint primitives. (L82-95) Links: docs/knowledge-types.md (5-type semantic ref), docs/initialization.md (init flow), docs/roadmap.md (v2.x direction), CHANGELOG.md (release history). (L97-110) Status badge + license + repo link + contributing. Replace ALL rc.1 placeholder content.

implementation: Read current README.md (120 lines) to capture any content that should be preserved (license, badge, contributing link)

Read packages/cli/package.json + packages/server/package.json + packages/shared/package.json to confirm package names + version (currently 2.0.0-rc.1; will become 2.0.0 in TASK-010 — README should say 'v2.0' not '2.0.0-rc.1')

Draft tagline + pain-point opening (3-5 sentences). Style: present-tense, direct, no marketing fluff. Target audience: AI delivery engineers + frontend/backend devs setting up Claude Code / Cursor / Codex. Concrete pain: rule walls fragment, knowledge dies in chat history, multi-client teams can't share context

Draft ASCII architecture diagram. Constraints: fits 80-column terminal, renders in any markdown viewer (no special unicode), shows 3 clients as boxes, MCP server in middle, .fabric tree on right with all dirs. Reference: existing Fabric architecture docs at docs/CODEBASE_LANDSCAPE.md or docs/ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md if a diagram exists. Diagram appears within first 100 words after tagline

Draft 'Why Fabric' section. Metaphor evolution: rule-binder (v1.x .fabric/rules/ era — static text, brittle) → AGENTS.md (mid-2025 era — single file, no lifecycle) → knowledge-sustainment (v2.0 — typed entries, decay, review, lint, audit). What Fabric does differently bullets: typed knowledge (5 types), maturity tiers (3 levels), dual-root (team + personal), audit trail (events.jsonl), lifecycle moat (lint + decay), MCP-first cross-client (Claude Code + Cursor + Codex)

Draft Quick Start section. Single command: `pnpm dlx @fenglimg/fabric-cli init`. After-init verify: `fabric doctor` (should report 0 fixable findings on a fresh init). One-line note about supported clients

Draft What you get section. 4 MCP tools (with one-line description each), 3 Skills (with one-line description each), Stop hooks (mention 3 client surfaces), dual-root layout (team in repo, personal at $FABRIC_HOME/.fabric/), lifecycle/lint (decay thresholds, archive policy)
Draft Links section. 4 doc links + CHANGELOG.md. Use relative paths (./docs/knowledge-types.md format)
Draft Status section. Preserve any existing badge / license note / repo URL from old README. Add 'v2.0 stable' release note when TASK-010 ships
Verify total length 150-200 lines, fits readable scroll-without-scroll on typical viewport
Verify no contradictions vs handoff.json or planning-context.md (e.g., do not claim semantic search; that's v2.x)
Run markdown linter if available (markdownlint --config .markdownlint.json README.md or equivalent) — 0 errors
Commit as 'docs(readme): full v2.0 narrative — replace rc.1 placeholder banner (rc.4 TASK-007)'

reference.pattern: Existing project README.md style for badges/license; v2.0 narrative content draws from handoff.json + planning-context.md key_findings + decision_context
reference.examples: Karpathy gist style for terse architecture; existing high-quality OSS readmes (e.g., dbt, Astro) for status/quick-start sections

criteria: README.md L1 is exactly `# Fabric — cross-client knowledge for AI agents.` (B' tagline verbatim)
Pain-point opening section is 3-5 sentences (verified by sentence-count grep)
ASCII architecture diagram appears in first 100 words after tagline (verified by word-count of pre-diagram body)
ASCII diagram shows 3 clients (Claude Code / Cursor / Codex) labeled explicitly
ASCII diagram shows MCP server box
ASCII diagram shows .fabric tree with directories: knowledge/, pending/, .archive/ (or fragment of)
ASCII diagram references metadata files: agents.meta.json, events.jsonl
Why Fabric section explains 3-era metaphor evolution: rule-binder, AGENTS.md, knowledge-sustainment
Quick Start section contains exactly the command `pnpm dlx @fenglimg/fabric-cli init` (verified by exact-string grep)
Links section contains relative links to docs/knowledge-types.md, docs/initialization.md, docs/roadmap.md, CHANGELOG.md (4 links, verified by grep)
rc.1 5-line v2 banner content (commit fab90d4) is fully removed (no traces of placeholder text)
No claims of unshipped features (no semantic search, no federated teams, no team-knowledge.git in v2.0)

rationale.chosen_approach: Full file rewrite (not incremental patch); rc.1 placeholder is acknowledged temporary; v2.0 deserves fresh narrative; all features present in main support truthful description
alternatives_considered: Incremental patch atop rc.1 banner — rejected: placeholder structure is misleading; full rewrite produces cleaner result
Marketing-style README with hero image + GIF demo — rejected: project is text-first developer tool; ASCII diagram is more honest
Skip ASCII diagram, use external SVG — rejected: GitHub renders ASCII fine; SVG asset adds repo weight without clarity gain
Multi-language README (Chinese + English) — rejected: project Chinese localization happens elsewhere (anomaly fabric-cn-localization session); README stays English
decision_factors: Handoff explicit: 'README rewritten with B' tagline; pain-point opening; ASCII architecture diagram in first 100 words'
v2.0 stable release deserves narrative anchor
Future docs (knowledge-types, initialization, roadmap) need a hub — README is that hub
rationale.tradeoffs: Full rewrite invites bikeshedding on prose; mitigated by handoff acceptance criteria specifying tagline + section requirements; iterations OK in TASK-010 if Gemini review flags issues

manual_checks: Render README.md in a markdown viewer (GitHub preview, VSCode, etc.); verify ASCII diagram is monospace-clean and not corrupted
Read top-to-bottom; verify tagline → pain-point → diagram → why → quickstart → what-you-get → links → status flows logically
Verify all relative links resolve (docs/knowledge-types.md, etc. — note: knowledge-types.md is created in TASK-008; verify after both tasks ship)
Verify no marketing fluff or unsubstantiated claims
success_metrics: 0 broken relative links to existing/scheduled docs

risks.description: ASCII diagram may render differently across viewers (GitHub, VS Code, terminal cat); column alignment may break
risks.mitigation: Wrap diagram in ```text fenced code block; test render in 3 viewers (GitHub web preview, VS Code preview, terminal cat); use 80-column width max; avoid box-drawing unicode in favor of ASCII +/-/| chars
risks.fallback: If unicode rendering causes issues, switch to plain ASCII-only chars

risks.description: Pain-point opening tone may not resonate with all readers (some find it negative)
risks.mitigation: Pair pain with Fabric's answer in same paragraph; keep tone factual not melodramatic; iterate in TASK-010 if Gemini review flags

risks.description: Links to docs/* may break if TASK-008 paths or filenames change
risks.mitigation: TASK-008 explicitly creates docs/knowledge-types.md; cross-reference TASK-008 file list before authoring README links; resolve in dogfood TASK-009
risks.fallback: Patch README link if TASK-008 deviates

key_functions.purpose: Tagline + pain + architecture diagram + why + quickstart + what-you-get + links + status
